exercises.title: Identify mutation operators at work
exercises.instructions:
PITest introduced these mutations. For each, say what was changed and whether a good test catches it (kills it).

Original: `if (amount > 0) { charge(); }`

Mutation A: `if (amount >= 0) { charge(); }`
Mutation B: `if (amount < 0) { charge(); }`
Mutation C: `if (true) { charge(); }`
Mutation D: (removed `charge()` call entirely)
exercises.expected_solution:
Mutation A: `amount > 0` → `amount >= 0`  (ConditionalBoundary operator)
   Change: the boundary condition is relaxed — amount=0 now charges
   Killed by: test asserting `charge()` NOT called when amount=0
   If no such test: SURVIVES → test gap discovered!

Mutation B: `amount > 0` → `amount < 0`  (NegateConditionals operator)
   Change: logic completely inverted — charges for negative, skips positive
   Killed by: any test verifying charge() called for positive amount
   Likely killed: most tests would catch this obvious inversion

Mutation C: `amount > 0` → `true`  (always-true)
   Change: condition removed — always charges regardless of amount
   Killed by: test asserting charge() NOT called when amount <= 0
   If only happy-path tests: SURVIVES

Mutation D: `charge()` call removed  (VoidMethodCall operator)
   Change: charge action deleted entirely
   Killed by: any test verifying charge() was called
   If testing only return value (not side effects): SURVIVES

test_cases.expected:
Mutation A: `amount > 0` → `amount >= 0`  (ConditionalBoundary operator)
   Change: the boundary condition is relaxed — amount=0 now charges
   Killed by: test asserting `charge()` NOT called when amount=0
   If no such test: SURVIVES → test gap discovered!

Mutation B: `amount > 0` → `amount < 0`  (NegateConditionals operator)
   Change: logic completely inverted — charges for negative, skips positive
   Killed by: any test verifying charge() called for positive amount
   Likely killed: most tests would catch this obvious inversion

Mutation C: `amount > 0` → `true`  (always-true)
   Change: condition removed — always charges regardless of amount
   Killed by: test asserting charge() NOT called when amount <= 0
   If only happy-path tests: SURVIVES

Mutation D: `charge()` call removed  (VoidMethodCall operator)
   Change: charge action deleted entirely
   Killed by: any test verifying charge() was called
   If testing only return value (not side effects): SURVIVES

hints: ConditionalBoundary: > becomes >=, < becomes <=
NegateConditionals: > becomes <=, == becomes !=
VoidMethodCall: removes void method call (side effects must be verified)

exercises.title: Complete the PITest Maven plugin configuration
exercises.instructions: Fill in the PITest plugin config to mutate the payment validation classes.

blanks.hint: prefix for the classes to mutate
prefix for the test classes to run
minimum % of mutations that must be killed (build fails below this)

hints: targetClasses: production classes to introduce mutations into
targetTests: test classes to run against mutations
mutationThreshold: build fails if mutation score below this %

exercises.title: Write tests that kill boundary mutations
exercises.instructions:
PaymentValidator.validate(amount): throws if amount <= 0 or > 1_000_000.

Current test only covers amount=500 (happy path). Write BOUNDARY tests that kill PITest mutations:
1. Test: amount=0 → throws
2. Test: amount=1 → valid (boundary just inside)
3. Test: amount=1_000_000 → valid (upper boundary)
4. Test: amount=1_000_001 → throws
5. @SpringBootTest: all 4 pass
hints: Boundary tests: test exactly AT the boundary and ONE step beyond
amount=0 kills: `<= 0` mutated to `< 0`

exercises.title: Interpret PITest HTML report
exercises.instructions:
PITest ran and produced these results for PaymentService:

```
Generated mutations: 24
Killed mutations:    18
Survived mutations:  6
Mutation score:      75%

Surviving mutations:
  Line 23: changed conditional boundary (>= to >)
  Line 31: removed call to auditLogger.log()
  Line 45: negated conditional (== to !=)
  Line 52: changed return value (empty to '[]')
  Line 67: removed call to notificationService.send()
  Line 78: changed conditional boundary (<= to <)
```

For each surviving mutation, explain: what test is missing?
exercises.expected_solution:
Line 23: changed `>=` to `>`
  Missing test: value exactly AT the boundary (e.g., amount == minimum threshold)
  Fix: add test asserting behavior when value equals boundary exactly

Line 31: removed `auditLogger.log()` call
  Missing test: verification that audit log is called
  Fix: `verify(auditLogger).log(any())` using Mockito
  Or: assert audit log entry exists in DB after operation

Line 45: negated conditional (`==` to `!=`)
  Missing test: the equality case (value exactly equals the checked value)
  Fix: add test where the condition is exactly true, verify correct branch

Line 52: changed return value to `[]` (empty list)
  Missing test: assertion on the actual return value content
  Fix: `assertFalse(result.isEmpty())` or `assertEquals(expectedList, result)`
  Test was likely only calling the method without asserting the result

Line 67: removed `notificationService.send()` call
  Missing test: verification that notification was sent
  Fix: `verify(notificationService).send(any())` using Mockito
  Or: check notification table/queue after the operation

Line 78: changed `<=` to `<`
  Missing test: value exactly AT the upper boundary
  Fix: test asserting behavior when value equals the upper limit exactly

Overall: mutation score 75% < typical target 80%. Need ~2 more mutations killed.

test_cases.expected:
Line 23: changed `>=` to `>`
  Missing test: value exactly AT the boundary (e.g., amount == minimum threshold)
  Fix: add test asserting behavior when value equals boundary exactly

Line 31: removed `auditLogger.log()` call
  Missing test: verification that audit log is called
  Fix: `verify(auditLogger).log(any())` using Mockito
  Or: assert audit log entry exists in DB after operation

Line 45: negated conditional (`==` to `!=`)
  Missing test: the equality case (value exactly equals the checked value)
  Fix: add test where the condition is exactly true, verify correct branch

Line 52: changed return value to `[]` (empty list)
  Missing test: assertion on the actual return value content
  Fix: `assertFalse(result.isEmpty())` or `assertEquals(expectedList, result)`
  Test was likely only calling the method without asserting the result

Line 67: removed `notificationService.send()` call
  Missing test: verification that notification was sent
  Fix: `verify(notificationService).send(any())` using Mockito
  Or: check notification table/queue after the operation

Line 78: changed `<=` to `<`
  Missing test: value exactly AT the upper boundary
  Fix: test asserting behavior when value equals the upper limit exactly

Overall: mutation score 75% < typical target 80%. Need ~2 more mutations killed.

hints: VoidMethodCall survivor: need verify(mock).method() to kill it
ConditionalBoundary survivor: need boundary-value test to kill it
Return value survivor: need assertion on what the method returns

exercises.title: Kill VoidMethodCall mutations with Mockito verify
exercises.instructions:
PaymentProcessor.process() calls:
1. validator.validate(amount)
2. auditLogger.log(merchantId, amount)
3. notificationService.send(merchantId)

Current test only asserts return value. Add Mockito verify calls to kill VoidMethodCall mutations:
1. verify validator was called
2. verify auditLogger was called with correct args
3. verify notificationService was called
4. @SpringBootTest: all 3 verify assertions pass
hints: verify(mock).method(args): kills VoidMethodCall mutation — proves the call happened
verifyNoInteractions(mock): proves a method was NOT called (for negative path)
Exact argument matching: verify(logger).log("MRC-001", 5000L) — stronger than verify(logger).log(any())

exercises.title: Mutation score threshold in CI pipeline
exercises.instructions:
Write a PITest Maven plugin configuration for CI:
1. mutationThreshold: 80 (build fails below 80%)
2. coverageThreshold: 70 (must have 70% line coverage)
3. Target: Razorpay payment validation classes
4. Exclude: generated code and DTOs
5. Explain: what happens in CI when threshold is breached
exercises.expected_solution:
<!-- pom.xml PITest plugin configuration for CI -->
<plugin>
  <groupId>org.pitest</groupId>
  <artifactId>pitest-maven</artifactId>
  <version>1.15.0</version>
  <dependencies>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.pitest</groupId>
      <artifactId>pitest-junit5-plugin</artifactId>
      <version>1.2.1</version>
    </dependency>
  </dependencies>
  <configuration>
    <!-- Production classes to mutate: -->
    <targetClasses>
      <param>com.razorpay.payment.validator.*</param>
      <param>com.razorpay.payment.service.*</param>
    </targetClasses>

    <!-- Test classes to run against mutations: -->
    <targetTests>
      <param>com.razorpay.payment.*Test</param>
      <param>com.razorpay.payment.*Spec</param>
    </targetTests>

    <!-- Exclude generated code and POJOs (no logic to mutate): -->
    <excludedClasses>
      <param>com.razorpay.payment.dto.*</param>
      <param>com.razorpay.payment.generated.*</param>
    </excludedClasses>

    <!-- CI thresholds: -->
    <mutationThreshold>80</mutationThreshold>   <!-- Build fails if <80% killed -->
    <coverageThreshold>70</coverageThreshold>   <!-- Build fails if <70% line coverage -->

    <!-- Performance: don't run on every build (slow): -->
    <skip>${skipMutationTests}</skip>   <!-- Skip with -DskipMutationTests=true -->

    <!-- Mutation operators: -->
    <mutators>
      <mutator>DEFAULTS</mutator>   <!-- Standard operators: boundary, negate, void, return -->
    </mutators>
  </configuration>
  <executions>
    <execution>
      <id>pitest</id>
      <phase>test</phase>
      <goals><goal>mutationCoverage</goal></goals>
    </execution>
  </executions>
</plugin>

<!--
CI BEHAVIOR WHEN THRESHOLD BREACHED:
  mutationThreshold=80, actual score=74%:
  [ERROR] BUILD FAILURE
  [ERROR] Mutation score 74 is below threshold of 80
  Jenkins/GitHub Actions: fails the build
  Developer: must write more tests before merge

  Good CI workflow:
  1. PR opened → mvn test (fast, no mutation)
  2. Merge to main → mvn test -Ppitest (mutation testing)
  3. Build fails → dev informed → must add tests
  4. -DskipMutationTests=true → skip for quick local builds
-->

public class PitestCIDoc { public static void main(String[] a) {
    System.out.println("PITest CI: build fails below 80% mutation score");
} }

expected_stdout_contains: PITest CI: build fails below 80% mutation score

hints: mutationThreshold: build fails if fewer than X% of mutations killed
skipMutationTests=true: skip in local dev (pitest is slow, runs all tests per mutation)
excludedClasses: skip DTOs and generated code — no business logic to mutate

exercises.title: High-value mutation target: payment routing logic
exercises.instructions:
PaymentRouter.route(amount, merchantTier): returns gateway based on logic:
- amount > 100_000 AND tier == PREMIUM → HDFC_PREMIUM
- amount > 100_000 → VISA_ENTERPRISE
- tier == PREMIUM → HDFC_STANDARD
- else → VISA_STANDARD

Write tests achieving 100% mutation score (kill ALL mutations):
1. All 4 branches tested
2. Boundary values for amount (100_000 and 100_001)
3. Both tier values
4. @SpringBootTest: 6+ tests, all pass
hints: Branch coverage ≠ mutation coverage: must test exact boundary values too
100_000 is NOT > 100_000 → goes to branch 3 or 4
100_001 IS > 100_000 → goes to branch 1 or 2

exercises.title: Surviving mutants: diagnosing weak tests
hints: >=10 mutated to >10: needs test at exactly 10 to catch it
>=5 mutated to >5: needs test at exactly 5 to catch it
Pattern: test at the exact boundary value to kill ConditionalBoundary mutations

exercises.title: Equivalent mutants — when survival is acceptable
exercises.starter_code: // Analysis of equivalent vs real surviving mutations
hints: Equivalent mutant: changes code but not behavior (genuinely can't be killed)
log.debug survivor: truly equivalent — debug log has no functional effect
Financial code: even tiny number differences are bugs — never equivalent

exercises.title: Mutation score calculation
exercises.instructions:
PITest ran against Razorpay PaymentService with these results:

```
Total mutations generated: 50
Killed (test detected change):  37
Survived (test missed change):  8
Timed out (infinite loops):     2
No coverage (not reached):      3
```

1. What is the mutation score?
2. Does the build pass with threshold=80%?
3. How many more mutations must be killed to reach 80%?
4. Should 'timed out' count as killed?
5. Should 'no coverage' affect the score?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Mutation score calculation:
   Score = killed / (total - no_coverage) × 100
   Score = 37 / (50 - 3) × 100
   Score = 37 / 47 × 100
   Score = 78.7% ≈ 79%
   Note: 'No coverage' excluded — can't kill what you can't reach
   'Timed out' (2): typically counted as killed (mutation caused infinite loop = detected!)
   With timeouts as killed: (37 + 2) / 47 × 100 = 83.0%

2. Build passes with threshold=80%?
   Without timeouts as killed: 79% < 80% → BUILD FAILS
   With timeouts as killed: 83% > 80% → BUILD PASSES
   PITest default: timeouts counted as killed
   So: 83% → BUILD PASSES

3. Mutations needed to reach 80% (without timeouts):
   Need: 80% of 47 = 37.6 → 38 killed
   Current: 37 killed
   Need 1 more mutation killed to reach 80%

4. Should timeouts count as killed?
   YES — a timeout means the mutation caused an infinite loop or very slow execution
   This IS a behavior change detected by the test (even if indirectly)
   PITest default: timeouts = killed
   Rationale: test suite 'detected' the mutation (by running forever) = caught it

5. Should 'no coverage' affect the score?
   NO — mutations in unreachable code can never be killed
   Including them would unfairly penalize the score
   PITest: excludes 'no coverage' from the denominator
   Fix: increase code coverage so those 3 mutations become reachable

test_cases.expected:
1. Mutation score calculation:
   Score = killed / (total - no_coverage) × 100
   Score = 37 / (50 - 3) × 100
   Score = 37 / 47 × 100
   Score = 78.7% ≈ 79%
   Note: 'No coverage' excluded — can't kill what you can't reach
   'Timed out' (2): typically counted as killed (mutation caused infinite loop = detected!)
   With timeouts as killed: (37 + 2) / 47 × 100 = 83.0%

2. Build passes with threshold=80%?
   Without timeouts as killed: 79% < 80% → BUILD FAILS
   With timeouts as killed: 83% > 80% → BUILD PASSES
   PITest default: timeouts counted as killed
   So: 83% → BUILD PASSES

3. Mutations needed to reach 80% (without timeouts):
   Need: 80% of 47 = 37.6 → 38 killed
   Current: 37 killed
   Need 1 more mutation killed to reach 80%

4. Should timeouts count as killed?
   YES — a timeout means the mutation caused an infinite loop or very slow execution
   This IS a behavior change detected by the test (even if indirectly)
   PITest default: timeouts = killed
   Rationale: test suite 'detected' the mutation (by running forever) = caught it

5. Should 'no coverage' affect the score?
   NO — mutations in unreachable code can never be killed
   Including them would unfairly penalize the score
   PITest: excludes 'no coverage' from the denominator
   Fix: increase code coverage so those 3 mutations become reachable

hints: Score denominator: total - no_coverage (not total)
Timeouts: counted as killed by PITest (mutation detected)
38 needed to reach 80%: 38/47 = 80.9%

exercises.title: Complete payment validation with 85%+ mutation score
exercises.instructions:
Write PaymentService.createPayment() and tests achieving 85%+ mutation score:
1. Validate: amount (1-1_000_000), merchantId not blank, gateway in {HDFC,VISA,RUPAY}
2. 3 separate validation checks
3. Tests: happy path + all 3 failure branches + all boundary values
4. @SpringBootTest: 8+ tests pass
hints: Boundary tests at 0, 1, 1_000_000, 1_000_001: kill ConditionalBoundary mutations
Test null AND blank separately: two different code paths
Test all valid gateway values: kills NegateConditionals mutations

exercises.title: PITest incremental mode for large codebases
exercises.instructions:
Design PITest incremental/scoped approach for Razorpay (large codebase, slow mutation runs):
1. Run PITest only on changed files in CI (not full codebase)
2. Scoped per-module: payment-service runs its own PITest separately
3. Maven profile: pitest-ci (active in CI only, skipped locally)
4. @SpringBootTest: show configuration strategy
exercises.expected_solution:
<!-- pom.xml: Incremental PITest strategy for large codebases -->

<!-- STRATEGY 1: Maven profile (CI only) -->
<!-- Run with: mvn test -P pitest-ci (CI activates profile automatically) -->
<profiles>
  <profile>
    <id>pitest-ci</id>
    <activation>
      <property><name>env.CI</name></property>  <!-- Auto-activate in CI -->
    </activation>
    <build>
      <plugins>
        <plugin>
          <groupId>org.pitest</groupId>
          <artifactId>pitest-maven</artifactId>
          <configuration>
            <targetClasses>
              <!-- Only mutate this module's classes -->
              <param>com.razorpay.payment.*</param>
            </targetClasses>
            <!-- Incremental analysis: only re-run if code changed -->
            <historyInputFile>${project.build.directory}/pitest-history.bin</historyInputFile>
            <historyOutputFile>${project.build.directory}/pitest-history.bin</historyOutputFile>
            <mutationThreshold>80</mutationThreshold>
            <!-- Parallel: speed up on CI multi-core -->
            <threads>4</threads>
          </configuration>
        </plugin>
      </plugins>
    </build>
  </profile>
</profiles>

<!-- STRATEGY 2: Per-module in multi-module project -->
<!-- payment-service/pom.xml: own PITest config -->
<!-- audit-service/pom.xml: own PITest config -->
<!-- fraud-service/pom.xml: own PITest config -->
<!-- Run per-module: cd payment-service && mvn pitest:mutationCoverage -->

<!-- STRATEGY 3: Skip locally, run in CI -->
<!-- Local: mvn test -DskipMutationTests=true (fast) -->
<!-- CI: mvn test (pitest activated by profile or env var) -->

<!-- PERFORMANCE TIPS:
  threads=4: parallel mutation testing (4 JVMs)
  historyInputFile: incremental — skip re-testing unchanged mutations
  timeoutConstant: limit how long each mutation run can take
  excludedMethods: skip trivial methods (getters, setters, toString)
-->

public class PitestStrategy { public static void main(String[] a) {
    System.out.println("PITest: CI profile + incremental history + per-module scope");
} }

expected_stdout_contains: PITest: CI profile + incremental history + per-module scope

hints: historyInputFile: incremental mode — skip unchanged mutations (huge speed boost)
threads=4: parallel execution (4 JVMs) — scales with CI machine cores
-DskipMutationTests=true: skip locally, enable in CI via profile/env var

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay mutation testing suite
exercises.instructions:
Build complete mutation-resistant test suite for payment routing:
1. PaymentRouter: 4-branch routing (large+premium, large, premium, standard)
2. Write tests killing ALL ConditionalBoundary and VoidMethodCall mutations
3. Use Mockito verify for side-effect mutations
4. @SpringBootTest + @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension): 8 tests, all pass
hints: verify(audit).recordRoute(exact, exact): kills VoidMethodCall (removal of audit call)
Boundary at exactly 100_000: kills ConditionalBoundary (> vs >=)
8 tests: 4 branches + 2 boundaries + 2 void-method verifications

exercises.title: Mutation testing for complex boolean logic
exercises.instructions:
Zerodha order eligibility check:
`eligible(age, kycCompleted, accountBalance, riskScore):`
- Must be: age >= 18 AND kycCompleted AND balance >= 10_000 AND riskScore <= 7

All 4 conditions must be true. Write tests that achieve near-100% mutation score:
1. Happy path (all true)
2. Each condition individually false (4 tests)
3. All boundary values for numeric conditions
4. @SpringBootTest: 9+ tests
hints: AND logic: each condition independently matters — test each false individually
Boundary at exactly 18, exactly 10_000, exactly 7: kills ConditionalBoundary
11 tests total for 4-condition AND: comprehensive mutation coverage

exercises.title: Complete M25 T3 Mutation Testing synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M25 T3 synthesis:
1. PaymentValidator with 3 checks (amount 1-100_000, merchantId, gateway)
2. Tests killing ALL mutations: boundaries, void calls, negations
3. Mockito verify for all side-effect calls
4. EquivalentMutantDoc: document which survivors are acceptable
5. @SpringBootTest + MockitoExtension (10 tests): all pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M25 T3 Mutation Testing synthesis
hints: 10 tests: happy + 3 fail branches + 3 boundaries + 2 audit verify + 1 audit no-interaction
verify(audit).log(exact, exact): kills VoidMethodCall removal mutation
verifyNoInteractions: proves audit not called on invalid input
